frontierComparison.interpretation: This assembly does not claim a global finite measure decrease. It consumes the immediate post-boundary decision obligation by naming the exact 13 q-bucket rank tokens and selecting one next-rank batch action before any singleton q-child descent.

assembledPieces.claim: The q-avoiding batch-cover packet classifies all 9,733,599 source q-avoiding classes across 718 source rows and leaves no source-row survivors.
assembledPieces.boundary: That source classification emits next rank obligations; it does not close the emitted root children or next q-avoiding rank classes.

assembledPieces.claim: The rank-boundary packet accounts for 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets q in {113,127,131,137,139,149,151,157,163,167,173,181,191}.
assembledPieces.boundary: The bucket partition is exact, but partitioning is not closure of any bucket.

assembledPieces.claim: Every stored source row has exactly two next roots modulo q^2, so the 9,733,599 source classes emit 19,467,198 next root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: The two-root law explains the finite successor shape; it does not prove those root children squarefree-repair, terminate, or globally cover.

assembledPieces.claim: The same 13 buckets emit 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding rank classes.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those classes remain outside the proof and need a batch cover, structural theorem, impossibility theorem, or sharper deterministic boundary.

supportedClaim: For the selected p443/q97/p479-available branch, the deterministic 13-bucket rank boundary is the whole live finite token after the q-avoiding batch cover. The next theorem step must cover or structurally reduce all 13 bucket tokens together; q113, q127, and other singleton child descent remain blocked until a later packet proves a smaller finite token is consumed.

nextFiniteToken.rankDecreaseIfCompleted: A batch cover, structural rank theorem, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic subboundary must account for every one of the 13 q-bucket tokens before any singleton descent.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_q_avoiding_13_bucket_next_rank_batch_cover
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 13 q-bucket tokens emitted by the deterministic rank boundary: 718 source rows, 9,733,599 source q-avoiding classes, 19,467,198 root children, and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The 13 buckets share the verified two-root successor law and were emitted by one no-survivor batch cover. A next-rank batch cover can test the whole rank surface for a common obstruction, structural split, or exact subboundary.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A singleton q113 or q127 descent would consume only one emitted bucket and would recreate the sibling-ladder anti-pattern after the frontier already expanded to 47 obligations.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no common batch theorem is visible, emit an exact deterministic per-bucket or subbucket rank-boundary packet that accounts for all 13 buckets and names the next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.exactBoundary: The next theorem surface is the 13 q-bucket rank family: 19,467,198 root children and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding classes.

oneNextAction.action: Derive a next-rank batch cover, structural rank theorem, or impossibility theorem over all 13 exact q-bucket tokens; if it fails, emit an exact deterministic per-bucket or subbucket boundary packet.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: All 13 q-bucket rank tokens q in {113,127,131,137,139,149,151,157,163,167,173,181,191}, covering 19,467,198 root children and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding classes.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: A deterministic ranked boundary packet with all 13 buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token; no singleton q-child descent is opened by this assembly.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The q-bucket family is already exact and small at the rank-token level. One batch screen over 13 tokens is cheaper and more theorem-facing than starting q113, q127, and sibling child repairs one at a time.
oneNextAction.completionRule: The next packet accounts for every one of the 13 q-bucket tokens by batch cover, structural theorem, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic subboundary.

proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the deterministic 13-bucket rank boundary. It chooses a whole-family next-rank batch action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted bucket, root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Run a next-rank batch cover or structural theorem attempt over all 13 exact q-bucket rank tokens; if no common theorem emerges, emit an exact deterministic 13-bucket subboundary instead of singleton q-child descent.
